name: Quarterly Activity Review
description: Quarterly review of sales team activity for sales-leadership audiences. Activity volume, mix, capacity utilization, top-performer patterns, structural learnings. Replaces the vague 'team is working hard' line in the QBR with a defensible narrative.
alsoSurfaces: What did sales actually do this quarter: volume, mix, capacity?
Where is the team over- or under-utilized?
What patterns distinguish the top quartile of reps?
Which playbook or capacity changes should we make next quarter?
beforeWeRun: Quarter to review: last completed, or custom?
Audience tone: sales leadership (analytical) or board-ready (concise)?

whatYoullGet.name: Executive summary
whatYoullGet.desc: 3–5 sentences: total activity volume, headcount, capacity utilization at team level, the single most important takeaway about execution.

whatYoullGet.name: Activity volume and mix
whatYoullGet.desc: Markdown table: activity types × review-quarter total, comparison-quarter total, delta, per-rep average. Plus narrative.

whatYoullGet.name: Capacity utilization
whatYoullGet.desc: Two paragraphs on team and segment-level capacity, headcount-vs-volume implications.

whatYoullGet.name: What changed
whatYoullGet.desc: 3–5 paragraphs on structural changes (playbook, process, team, sequences), with observed impact.

whatYoullGet.name: Top performer patterns
whatYoullGet.desc: Two paragraphs identifying patterns in the top quartile and whether those patterns are scalable.

whatYoullGet.name: Next quarter recommendations
whatYoullGet.desc: Playbook adjustments, capacity adjustments, experiments, each tied to specific data.

whatYoullGet.name: Sources & definitions
whatYoullGet.desc: Activity-type taxonomy, capacity assumptions, period boundaries, sources read with row counts.

callout: 4 quick inputs, then Sage drafts your activity review in ~16 min.
